story: I wake before dawn to my magic window and watch the Winder Dairy truck leave milk, eggs, and butter—comfort arriving in the dark. Lately small mercies hint money is tight; and my best friend, Tanya, has grown yellow and tired: leukemia. I pray for her, hold the glow of the milkman’s constancy, and decide that in the morning I’ll walk down to see her and maybe share my secret ritual. That same night, after a hard fever, Tanya’s mother decides to take her to the hospital at first light, leaving before visitors come.
implausible_knowledge: That same night, after a hard fever, Tanya’s mother decides to take her to the hospital at first light, leaving before visitors come.
question: When morning comes and she gets ready to visit Tanya, what will the narrator most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Ask her mother to drive her to the hospital.
answer_without_knowledge: Walk down the street to Tanya’s house as planned.
